extraction.one_line_summary: BAUHAUS Phil won the sprint after switching to the opposite side of the road when boxed in, timing his acceleration through traffic perfectly.
tactical_patterns: Bahrain Victorious worked prominently but BAUHAUS had to improvise road-side switch when boxed at 500m, suggesting leadout fragmentation.
Israel Premier Tech successfully controlled final 3km for NIZZOLO despite him finishing second, revealing best sprint train structure.
Sketchy corners between 3km-800m markers caused positioning chaos, punishing sprinters who stayed roadside sunny throughout the technical section.

rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: positive: demonstrated exceptional race craft by switching road sides when boxed, suggesting improved tactical maturity beyond pure power
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: involved in crash squeezing riders into barriers despite finishing 6th, indicates desperate positioning suggesting form concerns
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: tangled and missed out entirely despite being mentioned as big name, suggests declining ability to navigate technical finishes
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: positive: Israel's leadout functioned perfectly yet he still lost, revealing he may lack peak speed despite excellent positioning
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: neutral: controlled three jerseys but stage was service duty for ACKERMANN, confirming team hierarchy flexibility
